name: Node Offline
description: A StorageGRID node is completely offline and unreachable
check_query: SELECT node_name, node_type, status, last_seen FROM storagegrid_nodes WHERE status = 'offline'
condition: Any grid node reports offline status
impact: Data availability and redundancy compromised; ILM placement may fail for affected data
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > select affected node. 2. Check node status indicators and services. 3. Verify network connectivity (Grid Network, Admin Network). 4. SSH to node and run 'storagegrid-status' to check services. 5. Check power and hypervisor status for VM-based nodes. 6. Review /var/local/log/servermanager.log for errors. 7. If appliance, check BMC for hardware alerts. 8. Attempt service restart: 'storagegrid-service restart all'
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if node will not recover after basic troubleshooting, or if multiple nodes are offline simultaneously

name: Node Degraded
description: A StorageGRID node is online but reporting degraded services or health
check_query: SELECT node_name, node_type, services_status, alert_count FROM storagegrid_nodes WHERE services_status = 'degraded' OR health_status = 'degraded'
condition: Node services_status or health_status reports degraded
impact: Reduced redundancy and potential performance impact; some operations may fail or be slower
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > select affected node > Overview tab. 2. Check Services tab for individual service status. 3. Review active alerts on the node. 4. SSH to node: 'storagegrid-status' to list all services. 5. Restart degraded services: 'storagegrid-service restart <service-name>'. 6. Check resource utilization (CPU, memory, disk) under Hardware tab. 7. Review /var/local/log/ for service-specific error logs
support_case_note: Open support case if services will not restart or node remains degraded after service restarts

name: Storage Service Degraded (LDR/DDS)
description: Critical storage services (LDR or DDS/Cassandra) on a storage node are not running properly
check_query: SELECT node_name, service_name, service_status, last_restart FROM storagegrid_services WHERE service_name IN ('ldr', 'dds', 'cassandra') AND service_status != 'running'
condition: LDR or DDS service is not in running state on any storage node
impact: Object storage and retrieval operations are impaired; metadata queries may fail; data protection at risk
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > SUPPORT > Tools > Grid Topology > select node > LDR or DDS. 2. Check component health and alarm status. 3. SSH to node and check service: 'storagegrid-service status ldr' or 'storagegrid-service status dds'. 4. Attempt service restart: 'storagegrid-service restart ldr'. 5. For DDS/Cassandra issues, check Cassandra repair status and log files at /var/local/log/cassandra/. 6. Verify storage volumes are mounted: 'df -h /var/local/rangedb/*'. 7. If storage volumes are missing, recovery procedure may be needed
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case for persistent LDR/DDS failures or if Cassandra requires manual repair. Always engage support before running Cassandra recovery procedures

name: Admin Node Unreachable
description: Primary or non-primary Admin Node is not responding to management requests
check_query: SELECT node_name, node_role, status, management_ip, last_contact FROM storagegrid_nodes WHERE node_role = 'admin' AND status != 'connected'
condition: Admin node is not in connected status
impact: Loss of management plane; monitoring and alerting disrupted; audit logging may be interrupted; cannot make configuration changes
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Admin Node (if accessible from another admin node). 2. Attempt direct access via Admin Node management IP. 3. SSH to node: check network interfaces with 'ip addr show'. 4. Run 'storagegrid-status' to check all services. 5. Check NMS, MI, and management API services specifically. 6. Verify DNS resolution and Admin Network connectivity. 7. For primary Admin Node failure, assess if non-primary admin nodes can serve management functions. 8. If primary Admin Node requires recovery, follow recovery procedures with support guidance
support_case_note: Always open a support case for primary Admin Node failures. For non-primary Admin Nodes, open case if basic troubleshooting fails

name: Gateway Node Down
description: A Gateway Node providing S3/Swift API load balancing is offline
check_query: SELECT node_name, status, endpoint_count, active_connections, load_balancer_status FROM storagegrid_nodes WHERE node_role = 'gateway' AND status = 'offline'
condition: Gateway Node is offline
impact: Client S3/Swift API access may be disrupted if no other gateway nodes or load balancers are available; active connections dropped
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Gateway Node. 2. Check load balancer endpoint status: Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Network > Load balancer endpoints. 3. SSH to gateway node and check services: 'storagegrid-status'. 4. Restart load balancer service: 'storagegrid-service restart nginx-gw'. 5. Verify external load balancer health checks (if applicable). 6. Redirect client traffic to healthy gateway nodes via DNS or external LB. 7. Check for certificate issues on load balancer endpoints
support_case_note: Open support case if gateway node hardware has failed or if services will not restart after troubleshooting

name: Site Offline
description: An entire StorageGRID site is unreachable with all nodes at the site offline
check_query: SELECT site_name, COUNT(*) as offline_nodes, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM storagegrid_nodes n2 WHERE n2.site_name = n.site_name) as total_nodes FROM storagegrid_nodes n WHERE status = 'offline' GROUP BY site_name HAVING offline_nodes = total_nodes
condition: All nodes at a site are reporting offline status
impact: Site-level data unavailability; ILM placement failures for site-specific rules; potential data loss if single-site copies existed; cross-site replication halted
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > filter by site to confirm all nodes offline. 2. Check WAN connectivity to the site (contact Network Team). 3. Verify site power and physical infrastructure. 4. Check upstream network equipment (switches, routers, firewalls). 5. If site is permanently lost, initiate site decommission procedures with NetApp Support. 6. Monitor ILM re-evaluation for objects that had copies at the failed site. 7. Assess data loss exposure based on ILM policy (how many copies/EC fragments were at this site)
support_case_note: Always open Severity 1 case for site offline events. If site is permanently lost, NetApp Support must guide decommission procedure

name: Grid Federation Connection Failed
description: The connection between federated StorageGRID systems has failed
check_query: SELECT federation_name, connection_status, last_successful_sync, error_message, remote_grid_name FROM storagegrid_federation WHERE connection_status != 'connected'
condition: Grid federation connection is not in connected status
impact: Cross-grid replication halted; disaster recovery capability compromised; objects may not be replicated to remote grid
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > System > Grid federation. 2. Check connection status and error details. 3. Verify certificates have not expired on either grid. 4. Test network connectivity between grids (check firewall rules, port 443). 5. Verify DNS resolution of remote grid endpoints. 6. Check if remote grid is operational and accessible. 7. If certificates expired, renew and re-establish connection. 8. Check Grid federation audit logs for detailed error information
support_case_note: Open support case if connection cannot be re-established after verifying certificates and network connectivity, or if grid versions are incompatible

name: Expansion Node Pending
description: A node has been added to the grid expansion but has not been fully configured and joined
check_query: SELECT node_name, node_type, expansion_status, pending_since FROM storagegrid_expansion WHERE expansion_status = 'pending' AND pending_since < datetime('now', '-24 hours')
condition: Expansion node has been pending for more than 24 hours
impact: Planned capacity not available; investment in new hardware not being utilized; may delay capacity relief efforts
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Expansion. 2. Check expansion wizard for pending nodes and any validation errors. 3. Verify new node network configuration (Grid Network IP, Admin Network, Client Network). 4. Ensure new node can communicate with primary Admin Node. 5. Check new node hardware/VM status and resource allocation. 6. Review expansion prerequisites (correct StorageGRID version on new node). 7. Complete expansion wizard and approve pending node
support_case_note: Open support case if expansion wizard reports errors that cannot be resolved, or if node fails validation checks

name: Storage Node Capacity Warning
description: Storage node utilization has exceeded 85% of usable capacity
check_query: SELECT node_name, used_capacity_pct, total_capacity_tb, available_capacity_tb, object_count FROM storagegrid_capacity WHERE used_capacity_pct > 85 AND used_capacity_pct <= 95
condition: Storage node capacity utilization exceeds 85%
impact: Approaching capacity limits; ILM may fail to place new objects on this node; performance may degrade as storage fills
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Node > Storage tab. 2. Review Object Data Used vs. Object Data Allowed. 3. Check storage watermarks (soft/hard read-only). 4. Review ILM policies for potential optimization (compression, EC vs. replication). 5. Plan capacity expansion: Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Expansion. 6. Consider ILM lifecycle policies to expire or transition old objects. 7. Enable compression on storage pools if not already configured. 8. Check for and clean up any incomplete multipart uploads
support_case_note: Open support case for capacity planning assistance or if approaching hard read-only watermark

name: Storage Node Capacity Critical
description: Storage node utilization has exceeded 95% of usable capacity and is at imminent risk of exhaustion
check_query: SELECT node_name, used_capacity_pct, total_capacity_tb, available_capacity_tb, watermark_soft_read_only_pct FROM storagegrid_capacity WHERE used_capacity_pct > 95
condition: Storage node capacity utilization exceeds 95%
impact: Imminent storage exhaustion; node may enter read-only mode at soft watermark; S3 PUT operations will fail; ILM placement failures for new and existing objects
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Node > Storage tab — assess remaining capacity. 2. Immediately identify and delete any unnecessary objects or buckets. 3. Begin emergency capacity expansion if hardware available: Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Expansion. 4. Check storage watermark settings and adjust if necessary (temporary measure). 5. Review ILM rules for immediate space reclamation opportunities. 6. Temporarily reduce ingest rate if possible by coordinating with application teams. 7. Consider object migration to other storage nodes or sites with available capacity. 8. Contact NetApp for emergency capacity expansion guidance
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if any storage node is in read-only mode or capacity exceeds 98%. Open Severity 2 for capacity planning emergency

name: Metadata Capacity Warning
description: Cassandra metadata store on a storage node is approaching its allowed limit
check_query: SELECT node_name, metadata_used_pct, metadata_allowed_bytes, metadata_used_bytes, metadata_reserved_bytes FROM storagegrid_metadata WHERE metadata_used_pct > 70
condition: Metadata utilization exceeds 70% of allowed space
impact: Cannot store new objects even if object storage space is available; metadata capacity is bounded by node RAM and metadata volume size
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Node > Storage tab > Object Metadata section. 2. Review Metadata Used, Metadata Allowed, and Metadata Reserved. 3. Note: Metadata capacity cannot be increased by simply adding disks — it requires nodes with more RAM or dedicated metadata SSDs. 4. Plan addition of storage nodes with adequate metadata capacity. 5. Review object naming patterns — deeply nested paths and long key names consume more metadata. 6. Consider rebalancing objects across nodes. 7. Check for excessive object versioning consuming metadata space
support_case_note: Open support case for metadata capacity planning. NetApp can help assess metadata growth rate and recommend node specifications

name: Object Store Near Capacity
description: Overall grid object count or aggregate storage capacity is approaching platform limits
check_query: SELECT grid_name, total_objects, max_objects, object_count_pct, total_used_tb, total_capacity_tb FROM storagegrid_grid_capacity WHERE object_count_pct > 80 OR (total_used_tb / total_capacity_tb * 100) > 80
condition: Grid-wide object count or capacity exceeds 80% of limits
impact: Grid approaching maximum object count or capacity; new object ingestion may be throttled or fail
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Overview for grid-wide capacity view. 2. Review ILM policies for object lifecycle management. 3. Implement lifecycle expiration policies for data with known retention periods. 4. Plan grid expansion with additional sites or storage nodes. 5. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Expansion for capacity planning. 6. Consider erasure coding (EC) to improve storage efficiency vs. replication. 7. Review bucket policies for multipart upload cleanup rules
support_case_note: Open support case for grid-level capacity planning or if approaching documented platform limits

name: ILM Evaluation Queue Backlog
description: More than 10,000 objects are queued and awaiting ILM evaluation
check_query: SELECT queue_name, pending_count, oldest_pending_age_hours, evaluation_rate_per_sec FROM storagegrid_ilm_queue WHERE pending_count > 10000
condition: ILM evaluation queue contains more than 10000 pending objects
impact: Objects not being protected according to ILM policy; data protection gap; potential compliance risk for objects requiring specific placement
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > ILM > check ILM evaluation rate and queue depth. 2. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Nodes > check ILM evaluation metrics under Objects tab. 3. Check for ILM policy errors that may be stalling evaluation. 4. Verify all storage nodes are healthy and have available capacity. 5. Review ILM policy complexity — simplify rules if possible to improve evaluation rate. 6. Check for background operations (EC repair, rebalance) competing for resources. 7. If backlog continues to grow, add storage node capacity to improve evaluation throughput
support_case_note: Open support case if ILM backlog grows continuously despite healthy nodes and available capacity

name: Erasure Coding Repair Needed
description: Erasure-coded object data requires repair after a node or drive failure
check_query: SELECT repair_id, node_name, objects_pending_repair, repair_status, estimated_completion FROM storagegrid_ec_repair WHERE repair_status = 'needed' OR objects_pending_repair > 0
condition: EC repair is needed or objects are pending repair
impact: Reduced data protection for affected EC objects until repair completes; risk of data loss if additional failures occur before repair finishes
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Recovery > check EC repair status. 2. If repair not started, initiate: SSH to admin node and run 'repair-data start-ec-node-repair --nodes <node-id>'. 3. Monitor repair progress: 'repair-data show-ec-repair-status'. 4. Ensure target storage nodes have adequate capacity for repaired fragments. 5. Do not decommission or power off additional nodes while repair is in progress. 6. Monitor repair rate and estimated completion time. 7. If repair for volumes: 'repair-data start-ec-volume-repair --nodes <node-id> --volumes <vol-id>'
support_case_note: Open support case if EC repair stalls (no progress for >4 hours), fails with errors, or if multiple nodes in an EC scheme have failed simultaneously

name: S3 Request Latency Exceeds Threshold
description: Average S3 API request latency is exceeding 500ms, indicating performance degradation
check_query: SELECT node_name, avg_latency_ms, p99_latency_ms, request_rate_per_sec, error_rate_pct FROM storagegrid_s3_performance WHERE avg_latency_ms > 500
condition: Average S3 request latency exceeds 500 milliseconds
impact: Application performance degradation; S3 client timeouts; potential SLA breaches for dependent services
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > SUPPORT > Metrics (Grafana) > S3 Overview dashboard. 2. Identify which operations are slow (GET, PUT, HEAD, DELETE). 3. Check per-node performance to identify hot spots. 4. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Nodes > review CPU, memory, and disk utilization. 5. Review ILM policy complexity — complex policies slow evaluation. 6. Check network throughput between clients and gateway nodes. 7. Verify load balancer endpoint configuration and distribution. 8. Consider adding gateway or storage nodes to distribute load. 9. Review S3 client connection pooling and retry settings
support_case_note: Open support case if latency is consistently high with no identifiable cause, or if performance degradation correlates with a recent upgrade

name: High Object Retrieval Queue
description: Object retrieval requests are queuing on storage nodes, indicating a read performance bottleneck
check_query: SELECT node_name, retrieval_queue_depth, avg_retrieval_time_ms, concurrent_retrievals FROM storagegrid_retrieval WHERE retrieval_queue_depth > 100
condition: Retrieval queue depth exceeds 100 on any storage node
impact: Slow object downloads; application read latency increased; user experience degradation for data access
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Node > Objects tab > Retrieval metrics. 2. Check disk I/O performance: Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Node > Hardware tab. 3. Identify if specific volumes or rangedb mounts are slow. 4. Check for disk errors or SMART warnings on underlying storage. 5. Consider adding storage nodes to distribute read workload. 6. Review client access patterns for hot objects (consider CDN or caching layer). 7. For appliance nodes, check SSD cache or drive health via BMC
support_case_note: Open support case if retrieval queue depth is persistently high and disk I/O performance appears normal

name: Per-Node CPU Utilization Critical
description: A grid node is experiencing sustained CPU utilization above 90% for more than 15 minutes
check_query: SELECT node_name, cpu_usage_pct, node_type, sample_time FROM storagegrid_node_metrics WHERE cpu_usage_pct > 90 AND sample_duration_minutes > 15
condition: Node CPU utilization exceeds 90% for more than 15 minutes
impact: Service degradation on affected node; request processing delays; potential service failures or restarts
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > select node > Hardware tab > CPU utilization. 2. Identify top CPU-consuming processes: SSH to node and run 'top' or 'htop'. 3. Check if ILM evaluation, EC repair, or background tasks are consuming CPU. 4. Review recent workload changes that may have increased demand. 5. For VM-based nodes, verify vCPU allocation meets minimum requirements. 6. Consider rebalancing workload across nodes (add gateway/storage nodes). 7. If appliance node, verify thermal conditions are not causing CPU throttling
support_case_note: Open support case if high CPU is unexplained and not correlated with known workload or background operations

name: Per-Node Memory Utilization Critical
description: A grid node is experiencing sustained memory utilization above 90% for more than 15 minutes
check_query: SELECT node_name, memory_usage_pct, memory_total_gb, node_type FROM storagegrid_node_metrics WHERE memory_usage_pct > 90 AND sample_duration_minutes > 15
condition: Node memory utilization exceeds 90% for more than 15 minutes
impact: Risk of OOM (out of memory) conditions; services may crash or be killed; Cassandra performance degrades with insufficient memory
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > NODES > select node > Hardware tab > Memory utilization. 2. SSH to node: check memory usage with 'free -h' and 'top'. 3. Identify memory-consuming processes. 4. Check for memory leaks: is usage growing continuously? 5. For Cassandra/DDS nodes, verify Java heap settings. 6. Restart memory-leaking services if identified: 'storagegrid-service restart <service>'. 7. For VM-based nodes, consider increasing RAM allocation (requires node downtime). 8. For appliance nodes, memory upgrades may require hardware change
support_case_note: Open support case for persistent high memory utilization that returns after service restarts, or if Cassandra is experiencing OOM events

name: ILM Policy Not Active
description: No active ILM policy is configured, or the proposed policy has not been activated
check_query: SELECT policy_name, policy_status, last_activated, rules_count FROM storagegrid_ilm_policies WHERE policy_status = 'proposed' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM storagegrid_ilm_policies WHERE policy_status = 'active')
condition: No ILM policy is in active status
impact: Objects are not being managed according to any data protection policy; default placement may not meet business or compliance requirements
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > ILM > Policies. 2. Review proposed policy and ensure all rules are correctly configured. 3. Run ILM policy simulation to verify expected behavior: ILM > Policies > Simulate. 4. Check that all referenced storage pools and erasure coding profiles exist. 5. Activate the proposed policy: ILM > Policies > Activate. 6. Monitor ILM evaluation queue to ensure objects are being processed. 7. Verify the default rule provides adequate protection
support_case_note: Open support case if ILM policy activation fails or if objects are not being evaluated after activation

name: ILM Placement Unachievable
description: ILM cannot place objects according to configured rules due to missing storage pools or insufficient capacity
check_query: SELECT rule_name, error_type, affected_objects, since_time, storage_pool_name FROM storagegrid_ilm_errors WHERE error_type = 'placement_unachievable'
condition: ILM rules report placement unachievable errors
impact: Data not meeting protection requirements; regulatory compliance violations possible; objects may remain on ingest nodes without proper placement
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > ILM > check for active alerts about placement failures. 2. Review ILM rules: ILM > Rules — identify which rules have unachievable placements. 3. Verify referenced storage pools exist and have capacity. 4. If storage pool at a specific site is full, add capacity at that site. 5. Check if erasure coding profile requirements can be met (sufficient nodes). 6. Consider modifying ILM rules to use available storage pools as temporary measure. 7. Run ILM simulation to test proposed changes before activation
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case immediately — unachievable ILM placement means data is not meeting its protection requirements and may be at risk

name: Objects Pending ILM Evaluation Over 24 Hours
description: Objects have been waiting more than 24 hours for ILM evaluation and placement
check_query: SELECT COUNT(*) as pending_count, MIN(ingest_time) as oldest_pending, AVG(JULIANDAY('now') - JULIANDAY(ingest_time)) * 24 as avg_wait_hours FROM storagegrid_objects WHERE ilm_evaluated = false AND ingest_time < datetime('now', '-24 hours')
condition: Objects pending ILM evaluation for more than 24 hours exist
impact: Extended data protection gap; objects may have only interim protection (single copy on ingest node); compliance SLA at risk
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > ILM > check evaluation rate metrics. 2. Grid Manager > NODES > Storage Nodes > Objects tab for ILM evaluation rates. 3. Look for ILM policy errors or blocked evaluation queues. 4. Check storage node health — degraded nodes slow ILM evaluation. 5. Review ILM policy complexity (simplify where possible). 6. Verify storage pool capacity is sufficient for ILM placements. 7. Consider if recent large ingest is causing temporary backlog. 8. If persistent, add storage nodes to increase evaluation throughput
support_case_note: Open support case if objects remain unevaluated for more than 48 hours despite healthy storage nodes and available capacity

name: Cross-Grid Replication Lag
description: Replication between federated grids is falling behind the expected RPO target
check_query: SELECT federation_name, replication_lag_hours, pending_objects, last_replicated_time, bytes_pending FROM storagegrid_federation_replication WHERE replication_lag_hours > 4
condition: Cross-grid replication lag exceeds 4 hours
impact: Disaster recovery site not current; RPO (Recovery Point Objective) at risk; potential data loss exposure in disaster scenario
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > System > Grid federation — check replication status. 2. Verify connection health between grids (see sg-avail-007). 3. Check bandwidth utilization between sites — replication may be bandwidth-constrained. 4. Review pending object count and replication throughput rate. 5. Verify target grid has sufficient capacity for replicated objects. 6. Check for ILM policy issues on the target grid that may block incoming replication. 7. Consider QoS or bandwidth allocation for replication traffic. 8. If lag is extreme, may need to pause replication, resolve underlying issue, and resync
support_case_note: Open support case if replication lag exceeds SLA/RPO targets and cannot be resolved by addressing bandwidth or capacity constraints

name: TLS Certificate Expiring Soon
description: A TLS/SSL certificate used by StorageGRID will expire within 30 days
check_query: SELECT cert_name, cert_type, expiry_date, days_until_expiry, issued_to, issuer FROM storagegrid_certificates WHERE days_until_expiry BETWEEN 1 AND 30
condition: Certificate expires within 30 days
impact: When certificate expires, clients will be unable to connect securely; management access may be disrupted; S3 API calls will fail with certificate errors
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Security > Certificates. 2. Identify the expiring certificate type (Management interface, S3 API endpoint, or custom). 3. For CA-signed certificates: generate new CSR from Grid Manager, submit to your CA, and install renewed certificate. 4. For self-signed certificates: regenerate via Grid Manager (note: clients must update trust). 5. For S3 endpoint certificates: Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Network > Load balancer endpoints > edit endpoint > update certificate. 6. Plan certificate rotation during low-traffic period. 7. Update any clients or load balancers that pin or cache the certificate
support_case_note: Open support case only if certificate rotation causes unexpected issues or if Grid Manager certificate itself is expiring and cannot be accessed

name: TLS Certificate Expired
description: A TLS/SSL certificate used by StorageGRID has already expired
check_query: SELECT cert_name, cert_type, expiry_date, issued_to, issuer FROM storagegrid_certificates WHERE expiry_date < datetime('now')
condition: Certificate expiry date is in the past
impact: Active service disruption; clients cannot connect securely; S3 API calls failing; management interface may be inaccessible if management certificate expired
remediation_steps: 1. URGENT: Immediately replace the expired certificate. 2. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Security > Certificates (if accessible). 3. If Grid Manager is inaccessible due to expired management certificate, use the management API directly with certificate validation disabled, or access via CLI. 4. For emergency replacement, generate self-signed certificate to restore access, then plan CA-signed certificate deployment. 5. After certificate replacement, verify all client connections are restored. 6. Check load balancer endpoints for S3 API certificate issues. 7. Update monitoring to alert earlier on certificate expiry
support_case_note: Open Severity 1 case if management certificate has expired and Grid Manager is inaccessible — NetApp Support can assist with CLI-based certificate replacement

name: SSO Not Configured for Production
description: Single Sign-On (SSO) is not enabled on a grid classified as production environment
check_query: SELECT grid_name, sso_status, environment_type, local_user_count FROM storagegrid_security_config WHERE sso_status != 'enabled' AND environment_type = 'production'
condition: SSO not enabled on production grid
impact: Reliance on local user accounts for management access; no centralized identity management; weaker authentication compared to enterprise IdP; no MFA enforcement
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Access control > Single sign-on. 2. Select SSO type (SAML 2.0 recommended). 3. Configure identity provider (e.g., ADFS, Azure AD, Okta) with StorageGRID metadata. 4. Enable SSO in sandbox mode first to test without disrupting access. 5. Verify admin user group mappings from IdP to StorageGRID roles. 6. Test SSO login with multiple user accounts. 7. Switch from sandbox mode to enabled mode. 8. Ensure break-glass local root account password is securely stored for emergency access
support_case_note: Open support case if SSO configuration causes authentication lockout or if identity provider integration issues arise

name: Root User Password Age Exceeds Policy
description: The local root user account password has not been changed in over 365 days
check_query: SELECT grid_name, root_password_age_days, last_password_change FROM storagegrid_security_config WHERE root_password_age_days > 365
condition: Root password age exceeds 365 days
impact: Non-compliance with password rotation policies; increased risk from potentially compromised stale credentials; audit finding risk
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Access control > Root password. 2. Change the root password to a new strong password (minimum 16 characters recommended). 3. Securely store the new password in your organization's vault/secrets manager. 4. Update any emergency access runbooks with the new password (sealed envelope, break-glass procedure). 5. If SSO is enabled, root account should only be used for emergency access. 6. Document password change in change management system
support_case_note: Not applicable for password rotation. Open support case only if root account is locked out

name: Audit Logging Not Configured
description: External audit log forwarding (syslog) is not configured, limiting security audit trail
check_query: SELECT grid_name, audit_log_status, syslog_server, audit_level FROM storagegrid_audit_config WHERE syslog_server IS NULL OR syslog_server = ''
condition: No external syslog server configured for audit log forwarding
impact: No centralized audit trail; security incident investigation limited to local logs; compliance requirements for audit log retention may not be met; logs may be lost if node fails
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Monitoring > Audit and syslog server. 2. Configure external syslog server address and port (typically UDP 514 or TCP 6514 for TLS). 3. Select audit message categories to forward (recommend all categories for production). 4. Configure TLS for syslog transport if supported by your syslog infrastructure. 5. Set audit level appropriately (normal for most environments, verbose for high-security). 6. Test syslog delivery by generating a test event. 7. Verify log receipt on SIEM/syslog server. 8. Configure log retention policies on syslog server per compliance requirements
support_case_note: Not typically required. Open support case if audit logging is causing performance issues or if specific audit events are not being generated

name: StorageGRID Version Not Current
description: The running StorageGRID version is not the latest generally available release
check_query: SELECT grid_name, current_version, latest_version, version_age_days, hotfix_available FROM storagegrid_version WHERE current_version != latest_version AND eol_date IS NULL
condition: StorageGRID version does not match latest available version
impact: Missing security patches, bug fixes, performance improvements, and new features; may limit compatibility with newer client applications
remediation_steps: 1. Review release notes for the target version at NetApp Support Site. 2. Check upgrade path requirements (some versions require intermediate upgrades). 3. Verify hardware and software prerequisites: Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Software update > StorageGRID update. 4. Download StorageGRID upgrade package from mysupport.netapp.com. 5. Upload package to primary Admin Node. 6. Run upgrade prechecks. 7. Schedule maintenance window and notify stakeholders. 8. Execute upgrade (proceeds node-by-node automatically). 9. Monitor upgrade progress in Grid Manager. 10. Verify all nodes updated and grid healthy after completion
support_case_note: Open support case for upgrade planning assistance, especially for major version upgrades or large grids. Engage support if upgrade prechecks fail

name: StorageGRID Version End of Life
description: The running StorageGRID version has reached or is approaching end of support lifecycle
check_query: SELECT grid_name, current_version, eol_date, days_until_eol, support_status FROM storagegrid_version WHERE eol_date IS NOT NULL AND days_until_eol < 180
condition: StorageGRID version EOL within 180 days or already EOL
impact: No security patches or critical bug fixes available; NetApp Support may have limited ability to assist; compliance risk for regulated environments; insurance/liability implications
remediation_steps: 1. Immediately plan upgrade to a supported version. 2. Review NetApp Interoperability Matrix Tool (IMT) for upgrade path. 3. Download target version from mysupport.netapp.com. 4. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Software update > StorageGRID update. 5. Open proactive support case for upgrade planning and assistance. 6. Submit RFC to CAB with urgency justification (EOL timeline). 7. Plan and execute upgrade during approved maintenance window. 8. Verify grid health and all services operational after upgrade
support_case_note: Open Severity 3 proactive support case for upgrade planning. If already past EOL, emphasize urgency to NetApp Account Team

name: Appliance Firmware Outdated
description: StorageGRID appliance hardware firmware (SG5700, SG6000, SG6100, etc.) is not at the latest recommended version
check_query: SELECT node_name, appliance_model, current_firmware, latest_firmware, firmware_type FROM storagegrid_appliance_firmware WHERE current_firmware != latest_firmware
condition: Appliance firmware version does not match latest recommended version
impact: Missing hardware-level bug fixes, security patches, and stability improvements; may affect appliance performance or reliability
remediation_steps: 1. Download appliance firmware from NetApp Support Site (mysupport.netapp.com). 2. For SG5700/SG6000 (contains E-Series controller): Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Software update > SANtricity OS update. 3. For compute controller firmware: Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Software update > Linux updates or appliance-specific update path. 4. Review firmware release notes for known issues and prerequisites. 5. Schedule maintenance window — some firmware updates require node restart. 6. Apply firmware following NetApp-documented procedure for the specific appliance model. 7. Verify firmware version after update and check node health
support_case_note: Open support case if firmware update fails or if appliance does not return to normal operation after update

name: BMC Firmware Outdated
description: Baseboard Management Controller firmware on a StorageGRID appliance is not at the latest version
check_query: SELECT node_name, bmc_version, latest_bmc_version, appliance_model FROM storagegrid_bmc_firmware WHERE bmc_version != latest_bmc_version
condition: BMC firmware version does not match latest available version
impact: Missing IPMI security patches; remote management limitations; potential vulnerability to BMC-specific exploits
remediation_steps: 1. Download BMC firmware update from NetApp Support Site. 2. Access BMC management interface via dedicated BMC network port. 3. Log in to BMC web interface. 4. Navigate to firmware update section. 5. Upload and apply BMC firmware update. 6. BMC update typically does not require node restart but verify in release notes. 7. After update, verify BMC version and remote management functionality. 8. Update BMC credentials if recommended by firmware release notes
support_case_note: Open support case if BMC firmware update fails or if BMC becomes unresponsive after update. NetApp field service may need to perform BMC recovery

name: NTP Not Configured or Failing
description: NTP time synchronization is not configured or NTP servers are unreachable
check_query: SELECT node_name, ntp_status, ntp_server, time_drift_ms, last_sync FROM storagegrid_ntp WHERE ntp_status != 'synchronized' OR time_drift_ms > 500
condition: NTP status is not synchronized or time drift exceeds 500ms
impact: Time drift across nodes causes audit log inconsistency; S3 request signature validation may fail; ILM timing inaccurate; certificate validation issues possible
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Network > NTP servers. 2. Verify NTP servers are configured and reachable. 3. Ensure at least 4 NTP servers are configured for redundancy. 4. Check firewall rules allowing UDP port 123 from grid nodes to NTP servers. 5. SSH to affected node: 'ntpq -p' to check NTP peer status. 6. If NTP servers are internal, verify they are syncing to reliable time sources. 7. Monitor time drift after NTP configuration correction
support_case_note: Open support case if time drift is severe (>5 seconds) and cannot be corrected, as it may affect S3 authentication and audit logging integrity

name: DNS Configuration Missing or Invalid
description: DNS servers are not configured or DNS resolution is failing on grid nodes
check_query: SELECT node_name, dns_servers, dns_status, resolution_test_result FROM storagegrid_dns WHERE dns_status = 'failed' OR dns_servers IS NULL
condition: DNS resolution is failing or DNS servers are not configured
impact: Services requiring DNS resolution will fail; LDAP/AD authentication impacted; S3 endpoint resolution for clients may fail; external syslog forwarding disrupted
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > MAINTENANCE > Network > DNS servers. 2. Verify DNS server IP addresses are correct and reachable. 3. Test DNS resolution from grid nodes: SSH and run 'nslookup' or 'dig'. 4. Ensure firewall rules allow DNS traffic (UDP/TCP port 53). 5. Configure at least 2 DNS servers for redundancy. 6. Verify DNS servers can resolve both internal and external hostnames as needed. 7. Check for DNS server capacity issues if many grid nodes are querying
support_case_note: Open support case if DNS issues are causing S3 authentication failures or management access problems

name: SNMP Monitoring Not Enabled
description: SNMP agent is not enabled on the StorageGRID grid, limiting integration with enterprise monitoring tools
check_query: SELECT grid_name, snmp_enabled, snmp_version, trap_destinations FROM storagegrid_snmp_config WHERE snmp_enabled = false
condition: SNMP agent is not enabled on the grid
impact: No SNMP trap/notification delivery to enterprise monitoring systems; reduced visibility into grid health from NOC tools; manual monitoring overhead
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > Monitoring > SNMP agent. 2. Enable SNMP agent. 3. Configure SNMP community strings (SNMPv2c) or USM users (SNMPv3 — recommended for production). 4. Configure trap/inform destinations pointing to your monitoring platform. 5. Set system contact and location information. 6. Test SNMP connectivity from your NMS (e.g., snmpwalk). 7. Configure SNMP-based alerts in your monitoring platform using StorageGRID MIBs
support_case_note: Not required for SNMP configuration. Open support case only if SNMP agent is enabled but not sending traps as expected

name: Alert Notification Not Configured
description: Email or SNMP alert notifications are not configured, meaning alerts may go unnoticed
check_query: SELECT grid_name, email_configured, snmp_configured, notification_count FROM storagegrid_alert_config WHERE email_configured = false AND snmp_configured = false
condition: Neither email nor SNMP alert notifications are configured
impact: Critical alerts may not be noticed in time; reliance on manual Grid Manager checks; increased risk of missed incidents; slower incident response
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > ALERTS > configure email notifications: ALERTS > Email setup. 2. Add SMTP server configuration (server, port, credentials, TLS). 3. Configure alert notification recipients (individual emails or distribution lists). 4. Select which alert severities trigger notifications (recommend: all severities for production). 5. Send test email to verify delivery. 6. Alternatively or additionally, configure SNMP traps (see sg-conf-003). 7. Consider integrating with webhook or API-based alerting for ChatOps tools
support_case_note: Not required. Open support case only if notification delivery is inconsistent despite correct configuration

name: S3 Bucket Without Versioning or Object Lock
description: Production S3 buckets do not have versioning enabled, risking accidental data loss from overwrites or deletes
check_query: SELECT bucket_name, tenant_name, versioning_status, object_lock_enabled, object_count FROM storagegrid_buckets WHERE versioning_status != 'Enabled' AND environment_type = 'production'
condition: Production bucket does not have versioning enabled
impact: Accidental deletes or overwrites permanently destroy data; no ability to recover previous object versions; compliance requirements for data immutability may not be met
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > TENANTS > select tenant > S3 console or use S3 API. 2. Enable versioning on the bucket: PUT Bucket versioning with Status=Enabled. 3. For compliance/WORM requirements, enable S3 Object Lock on the bucket (must be set at bucket creation — cannot be added later). 4. Configure lifecycle policies to manage version count/expiration. 5. Review ILM rules to ensure versioned objects are properly placed. 6. Educate application teams about versioning implications (storage overhead, list behavior). 7. For new buckets requiring compliance, create with Object Lock enabled from the start
support_case_note: Open support case if S3 Object Lock configuration is needed and assistance is required, or if compliance requirements are unclear

name: S3 Object Lock Not Enabled for Compliance Buckets
description: Buckets tagged for compliance/regulatory retention do not have S3 Object Lock enabled
check_query: SELECT bucket_name, tenant_name, compliance_tag, object_lock_enabled, retention_mode FROM storagegrid_buckets WHERE compliance_tag IS NOT NULL AND object_lock_enabled = false
condition: Compliance-tagged bucket does not have S3 Object Lock enabled
impact: Regulatory non-compliance risk; data could be modified or deleted before retention period; audit finding for SEC 17a-4, HIPAA, or GDPR data retention requirements
remediation_steps: 1. Note: S3 Object Lock must be enabled at bucket creation time — it cannot be added to existing buckets. 2. If existing non-compliant bucket: Create new bucket with Object Lock enabled, migrate data, then remove old bucket. 3. For new buckets: Grid Manager > TENANTS > select tenant > create bucket with Object Lock. 4. Configure default retention settings (mode: COMPLIANCE or GOVERNANCE, period). 5. Ensure global S3 Object Lock setting is enabled: Grid Manager > CONFIGURATION > System > S3 Object Lock. 6. Review ILM policy for compliant placement rules. 7. Document compliance bucket inventory for audit purposes
support_case_note: Open support case for assistance with data migration from non-compliant to compliant buckets, or for compliance architecture review

name: Tenant Quota Not Set
description: A tenant account does not have a storage quota configured, allowing unlimited consumption
check_query: SELECT tenant_name, tenant_id, quota_gb, used_gb, object_count FROM storagegrid_tenants WHERE quota_gb IS NULL OR quota_gb = 0
condition: Tenant has no storage quota or quota is set to zero (unlimited)
impact: Single tenant could consume all available grid capacity; no chargeback or showback data; capacity planning undermined; other tenants may be impacted by resource exhaustion
remediation_steps: 1. Grid Manager > TENANTS > select tenant > edit. 2. Set storage quota appropriate for the tenant's allocation. 3. Consider: current usage, growth projections, and total grid capacity. 4. Enable quota alerts to notify tenant admins when approaching limits. 5. Review all tenants and establish quotas based on capacity planning. 6. Consider implementing tenant-specific ILM rules if different retention is needed. 7. Document quota assignments for chargeback purposes
support_case_note: Not typically required. Open support case for multi-tenancy best practice guidance or capacity planning assistance
